segments.text: Let's walk through a factorial function line by line and see exactly how it works.
Line one defines the function. It takes a single number n as input.
Line two is the base case check. If n is zero or one, we stop recursing.
Line three returns one for the base case — since zero factorial and one factorial both equal one.
Line four is the recursive case. We multiply n by the factorial of n minus one.
Now let's trace through an actual execution — factorial of five.
Factorial of five expands to five times factorial of four, then five times four times factorial of three, and so on.
We keep expanding until we reach factorial of one, which is just one. Now we can multiply everything together.
Five times four times three times two times one equals one hundred and twenty. That's factorial of five.
